title: Divergent Recommendations, Convergent Diagnoses: Cross-Provider Failure-Mode Convergence in AI Commercial Recommendation
abstract: arXiv:2606.26116v1 Announce Type: cross  Abstract: A brand whose customers use both ChatGPT and Claude for product recommendations faces a strategic choice: a single optimization playbook, or one per provider? Across 215 commercially-framed prompts in four measurement batches, the two providers disagree on which brands they recommend roughly two-thirds of the time (cross-provider recommendation Jaccard 0.35, below the 0.50-0.61 same-prompt rerun baseline). The picks diverge. But when neither provider recommends a brand, we classify the failure into one of three modes -- discoverability (the brand never reaches the model), compellingness (it reaches the model but isn't mentioned), or positioning (it's mentioned but not recommended) -- and on 7,763 such joint failures, both providers diagnose the same failure mode 95.1% of the time (clustered 95% CI [94.3%, 95.7%]). Agreement rises monotonically with falling brand prominence, from 81% [78.2%, 84.0%] on category leaders to 99.6% [99.3%, 9

Title: Divergent Recommendations, Convergent Diagnoses: Cross-Provider Failure-Mode Convergence in AI Commercial Recommendation

The picks diverge. translated_title: 分歧的推荐，趋同的诊断：AI商业推荐中跨提供商的失败模式趋同
translated_abstract: arXiv:2606.26116v1 公告类型：交叉 摘要：一个其顾客同时使用ChatGPT和Claude进行产品推荐的品牌面临着一个战略选择：是采用单一优化策略，还是为每个提供商分别制定策略？在四个测量批次中的215个商业框架提示中，两个提供商在它们推荐的品牌上约有三分之二的时间存在分歧（跨提供商推荐Jaccard系数为0.35，低于相同提示重跑基线0.50-0.61）。选择出现分歧。但当两个提供商都不推荐某个品牌时，我们将失败归类为三种模式之一——可发现性（品牌从未触及模型）、吸引力（品牌触及模型但未被提及）或定位（品牌被提及但未被推荐）——在7,763个此类联合失败中，两个提供商在95.1%的情况下诊断出相同的失败模式（聚类95%置信区间[94.3%, 95.7%]）。一致性随着品牌知名度的下降而单调上升，从类别领导者的81% [78.2%, 84.0%] 上升到99.6% [99.3%, 99.9%]。
tldr: 尽管ChatGPT和Claude在品牌推荐上存在高度分歧，但在不推荐品牌时，它们对失败原因的诊断却高度一致，且这种一致性随品牌知名度降低而增强。
en_tdlr: Although ChatGPT and Claude diverge significantly in brand recommendations, they diagnose the causes of failure with high agreement when neither recommends a brand, with consistency increasing as brand prominence decreases.
